title: Power-Sector Circular Debt

current.note: Ministry of Energy reported the power-sector circular debt flow/stock at about Rs1.85 trillion at end-April 2026; the IMF's estimate is slightly lower at ~Rs1.76 trillion.

yoy.note: Down about 23% from ~Rs2.41 trillion a year earlier — a genuine reduction in the stock.

fytdBuildup.note: Despite the lower stock, fresh circular debt of ~Rs240bn was added during FY2025-26 to date — up sharply from just ~Rs18bn in the same period a year earlier — driven by DISCO under-performance and K-Electric non-payments.

powerVsGas.note: Looking beyond electricity, the combined power + gas circular debt is far larger at ~Rs5.20 trillion (power ~Rs1.76tn, gas ~Rs3.44tn).

targets.label: FY2026
targets.detail: Government committed under the IMF programme to no net increase in circular debt by end-June 2026.
targets.statusNote: Rs240bn has already been added in the year to date.

targets.label: FY2027
targets.detail: Circular Debt Management Plan caps the annual gross flow at Rs300bn.

targets.label: FY2031
targets.detail: Medium-term goal to eliminate fresh circular-debt accumulation entirely.

reforms: Annual electricity tariff rebasing plus regular fuel/quarterly adjustments
Renegotiating power-purchase agreements with IPPs to lower capacity payments
Converting part of the stock into formal CPPA-G liabilities (bank financing)
Improving DISCO recoveries and curbing theft; resolving K-Electric dues
methodologyNote: Power-sector circular debt is the unpaid stock owed across the electricity supply chain (generators, fuel suppliers, IPPs). Figures are from the Ministry of Energy / Power Division and IMF, reported by Pakistani financial media. Power and gas figures are not directly additive across all reporting dates; targets are IMF-programme / Circular Debt Management Plan commitments and are subject to revision.
